ogTitle: Allenby Gardens vs Allendale East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Allenby Gardens and Allendale East.
heading: Allenby Gardens vs Allendale East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Allenby Gardens, South Australia and Allendale East, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Allendale East has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Allenby Gardens (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Allenby Gardens or Allendale East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Allenby Gardens has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Allendale East scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Allenby Gardens scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Allendale East scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Allenby Gardens or Allendale East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Allendale East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
